title: Thelypteris sambasensis
intro: Thelypteris sambasensis, also known as Sambas fern, is a species of plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris sambasensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tonics and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
taxonomy: Thelypteris sambasensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen ferns. The species is native to certain regions in the world and is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae. Thelypteris sambasensis is also known to be a part of the phylum Pteridophyta, which comprises of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The classification of Thelypteris sambasensis is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Pteridophyta, class - Pteridopsida, order - Blechnales, family - Thelypteridaceae, genus - Thelypteris, species - T. sambasensis.
